# Harness Engineering

> A nine-part conceptual guide to the software around a coding model, grounded in the Grok Build source.

Guide: https://artvandelay.github.io/grok-harness-explained/
Interactive source graph: https://artvandelay.github.io/grok-harness-graph/
Source code studied: https://github.com/xai-org/grok-build
Guide repository: https://github.com/artvandelay/grok-harness-explained

## Topics

- What an agent harness is
- Agent loops and durable sessions
- Tool schemas, dispatch, and action surfaces
- Permission policies and sandboxing
- Context assembly, compaction, memory, and workspace grounding
- Model sampling, streamed events, retries, and loop recovery
- MCP, ACP, subagents, and hooks
- Terminal rendering and run modes

The guide's deep source links are pinned to Grok Build commit a5727c5960452e7527a154b25cb5bf00cda0545e.
